summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, Games / criticism, Science / computing. The strongest items in this run are Manhattan DA’s office to investigate Eric Swalwell over sexual assault allegations, GTA 6 developer Rockstar Games hacked once again but insists this time only a "limited amount of non-material company information" was compromised, and Epic set to release Arc Raiders-like extraction shooter with Disney characters by the end of the year.

mainItems.summary: Democratic representative has denied accusations from former staffer as he faces calls to drop bid for governor The Manhattan district attorney’s office said on Saturday that it is investigating a sexual assault allegation against Eric…

mainItems.title: How AI is getting better at finding security holes
mainItems.summary: Anthropic announced this week that its new model found security flaws in "every major operating system and web browser." Even before the news, AI models had gotten dramatically better at finding bugs.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

mainItems.title: ‘Everything is gone’: Israel destroys entire villages in Lebanon
mainItems.summary: Rights groups fear tactic of ‘domicide’ trialled in Gaza, where entire areas are made uninhabitable, is being used again The Israeli military has demolished entire villages as part of its invasion of south Lebanon, rigging homes with…
mainItems.whyItMatters: Worth watching for authoritarian drift, selective enforcement, and democratic norms.

mainItems.title: Roblox is introducing a subscription service that encourages more shady practices, is it? Sounds about right
mainItems.summary: I truly wonder when enough will be enough for Roblox Corporation. We all know how much money Roblox is raking in, and yet yesterday it was announced that a shiny new subscription service called Roblox Plus is on the way.

mainItems.title: Your article about AI doesn’t need AI art
mainItems.summary: The illustration for The New Yorker's profile of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is a jump scare. Altman stands in a blue sweater with a blank expression.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to the shape of current AI systems and where the tooling is moving.

mainItems.title: The Cost of ICE Raids: Fewer Students, Less Money, Missing Parents
mainItems.summary: School (in)Security is our biweekly briefing on the latest school safety news. Subscribe here. Two recent stories by reporters here at The 74 demonstrate the ongoing ripple effects of the Trump administration’s massive deportation campaign.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: The Oldest Octopus Fossil Ever Isn’t An Octopus At All, Scientists Discover
mainItems.summary: A re-examination of a 300-million-year-old fossil that was long thought to be the earliest octopus revealed that the animal was actually part of the nautilus family.

mainItems.title: Missouri Child Care Subsidy Cuts Could Hit Foster Kids, Low-Income Families Hardest

youtube.title: Israeli airstrikes kill 3 more journalists in Lebanon and Gaza

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.
